title: How to Building a Crypto Store on GitHub Pages: The Complete Creator Guide — A Practical Breakdown
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: From zero to live crypto store in one afternoon. GitHub Pages hosting, Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, NOWPayments integration.

content.text: You're a digital creator in Ethiopia trying to sell your ebooks, courses, or tutorials online, but every payment platform says no to you due to banking limitations or KYC requirements. You're not alone. Many creatives face the same struggle. You need a way to accept payments and deliver your digital goods without relying on PayPal or Stripe. That's why you're here, to learn how to build a crypto store on GitHub Pages. You're in the right place.
Why This Method Works When Everything Else Fails

The beauty of using GitHub Pages and Unchained Commerce lies in its ability to bypass traditional payment gateways. Unlike PayPal or Stripe, Unchained Commerce doesn't require you to create an account or link your bank. Instead, you can focus on creating and selling your digital products without worrying about the payment hassle. This method works because it's designed for creators like you who want to monetize their content without the middleman.
What You Need Before You Start

To get started, you'll need a GitHub account, a basic understanding of HTML and CSS, and the Full Stack Bundle from Unchained Commerce. This bundle includes the Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, and Admin Dashboard, giving you a complete solution for setting up a crypto store. You'll also need to set up a NOWPayments account, which will serve as your payment processor. Don't worry if you're new to coding; the Full Stack Bundle comes with deployment guides and Discord support to help you along the way.
Setting Up Your Payment and Delivery Pipeline

Now it's time to set up your payment and delivery pipeline. You'll use the Core Shop Engine to create a seamless payment experience for your customers. The Web3 Wallet Connect frontend will allow your customers to connect their wallets and make payments. Meanwhile, the Admin Dashboard will give you real-time analytics and transaction logs to track your sales. The NOWPayments payment processor will handle all the payment processing, so you don't have to worry about a thing.
Don't rely on a single payment platform; diversify your revenue streams with multiple payment options.
Going Live and Making Your First Sale

Once you've set up your payment and delivery pipeline, it's time to go live. Deploy your crypto store to GitHub Pages and start promoting it to your audience. Use the Full Stack Bundle's deployment guide to ensure everything is set up correctly. When someone makes a purchase, the Core Shop Engine will automatically deliver the digital product, and the Admin Dashboard will log the transaction. You'll be earning money and delivering your digital goods in no time.
Scaling Once the System Is Running
How do I build a digital store that accepts crypto payments?

The correct setup sequence matters because each component depends on the previous one being configured correctly. Start with NOWPayments: create your account, add your wallet addresses for each coin you want to accept, and generate your API key. Then deploy the Core Shop Engine: upload your product files, configure your NOWPayments API credentials, set your product prices in USD, and deploy to your hosting environment. Then configure Web3 Wallet Connect: connect it to your Core Shop Engine endpoint, set up your product display, and test the checkout flow in a browser. Finally, complete a real mainnet test transaction with a small amount of actual crypto. Confirm the webhook fires, the engine processes it, the delivery email arrives, and the download link works. Only after that test passes should you start driving traffic to your store.
Keep your digital products organized and easily accessible with the Admin Dashboard's transaction logs.
Related: How Creators Like You Are Getting Paid

If you're new to coding, consider checking out our tutorials on GitHub Pages and Unchained Commerce. For more advanced creators, explore the world of smart contracts and decentralized finance (DeFi). The Full Stack Bundle is a great starting point for building a crypto store, but don't be afraid to experiment and try new things.
Your Action Checklist

Follow the setup sequence in order — skipping steps creates configuration errors that are hard to diagnose. First, create your NOWPayments account and add your wallet addresses and API key. Second, download and configure the Core Shop Engine with your NOWPayments credentials, product file paths, and delivery settings. Third, set up Web3 Wallet Connect with your product list and checkout configuration. Fourth, push everything to your GitHub repository and enable GitHub Pages on the repository. Fifth, configure your custom domain if you are using one — update DNS settings and confirm HTTPS is active. Sixth, complete a real mainnet test transaction and confirm the full pipeline: payment received, webhook fired, engine processed, receipt generated, delivery email sent, download link working. Seventh, check the Admin Dashboard entry for the test transaction. Only after step six and seven pass cleanly should you share your store URL publicly.
Use the Full Stack Bundle's deployment guide to ensure a seamless setup process and minimize technical issues.
Full Stack Bundle — $79.99. Everything — Core Engine, Wallet Connect, Dashboard — plus priority updates, Discord support, and deployment guide. Your complete store infrastructure, one payment.
